Month Story Estimate Analysis

For epics in the active analysis scope, single-month stories contribute directly. Stories spanning multiple months fall back to subtask estimates; if no usable subtasks exist, the epic is excluded and listed below. With all toggles off, the chart shows all available months. Through mode uses inclusive epic month overlap.

27User & role management JFintech FuelTo Do19290
28Station and tank configurationFintech Fuel—14467
29Product mappingFintech Fuel—11252
30Unit and currency settingsFintech Fuel—240112
31Mobile View without Manual submit DataFintech Fuel—344161

34NoneFintech Fuel—
35Calculate Tank delivery (Alsons device) JFintech FuelTo Do12056
36Unified Platform & Multi-Site Visibility (Consolidated view for all sites) - The client wants a unified platform that provides information from all plant sites in one place. - Key data points requested in the unified view include storage tank volume and timestamped deliveries. - The solution must support network-scale visibility for hundreds of sites, not just a small pilot set. - Different user levels, including management, leadership, supervisors, and operations teams, need different views of the same data. - Visibility of sites, targets, and data should follow the assigned user hierarchy.Fintech Fuel—20897
37Deliveries & Delivery Identification - Deliveries are already available with timestamps in Fintech Fuel. - The client wants to distinguish PGL deliveries from non-PGL deliveries, with a clear classification such as PGL and Not PGL. - At COCO sites, current deliveries are considered PGL deliveries. - At DODO sites, the client needs a way to identify who decanted product into storage because multiple providers may use the same storage. - The current level of control at DODO sites is weaker than at COCO sites, and the client wants better controls in Fintech Fuel. - The Deliveries page should use the industry term Delivery Note instead of Bill of Lading. - For imported products, a delivery note or lading reference should be captured on the UI. - A lading reference is currently seen as a reference point only and not as a full control mechanism. - The client wants this requirement captured as a KPI or future control area even if it is initially implemented as a wish-list item. - The system should highlight cases where actual decanted quantity exceeds expected quantity. - Manual delivery references alone are not sufficient to establish end-to-end control.Fintech Fuel—14467
38Dashboard Enhancements - Visual representations - Sales vs target trend must be dynamic not just last 7 days - Stock level must be dynamic not just last 7 days - Remove minute-level filters - Show time of fetched data on stock-level - positive/negative variance with color codingFintech Fuel—8841
39Historical Comparison & Trend Analysis - Comparison of different metrices like sales vs deliveries etc.Fintech Fuel—14467
40Reconciliation & Variance Analysis - Reconciliation is a key area for the client, especially to monitor external uplifting. - The reconciliation report should help users see how frequently variation occurs over time. - The client wants anomaly highlighting for variances above selected thresholds. - The current tabulated Excel-like representation is difficult to consume and needs a better UI/UX treatment. - The user wants a product-tile style interaction where clicking a product reveals its reconciliation details. - There is currently no way to define a threshold value in the system; the client asked for configurable selected thresholds. - The system should highlight anomalies at the time of reconciliation. - The reconciliation workflow should support investigation into why a difference exists. - Initial anomaly reasons should at least include data disconnectivity, whether due to internet issues or equipment malfunction. - The reconciliation report must show consistent data and clearly reflect when a site is not transmitting data. - If data is missing, the report should show an exclusion note so the variance can be interpreted correctly. - The client suggested a rule such as flagging any site where no data transmission happens for 60 or more minutes. - Capturing the anomaly event is considered the first priority; the detailed report presentation can be refined with users afterward. - The report should include a summary table on top for the selected period with one row per product, instead of only date-by-date product entries. - The user emphasized that reconciliation UI/UX quality is a possible road blocker.Fintech Fuel—272127
41Data Accuracy, Connectivity & Equipment Failure - Data accuracy is a major concern for the client. - Two primary failure scenarios were identified: -- Internet disconnectivity between the site and the cloud. -- Site hardware or controller malfunction. - In internet disconnectivity scenarios, the controller device must accumulate data and transmit it once connectivity is restored. - No data loss is acceptable when internet connectivity is interrupted. - If the controller or site equipment is not working, the system must raise an alert. - The client wants the system to inform users whether a site cannot reconcile because of internet disconnectivity or equipment malfunction. - If equipment fails and data is not captured, the system must provide manual entry for lost site data. - Manual entry is considered necessary to maintain 100% data accuracy and complete reconciliation. - The client requested an admin-level tool for manual data entry when ATG or related equipment is down. - The discussion referenced a practical case where if ATG is down for two hours, the system should allow offline or manual input. - Offline input data is therefore a required feature.Fintech Fuel—304142
42Alerts, Diagnostics & Notifications - Alerts should not remain isolated in one report; they should connect with diagnostics and related operational reports. - Email notifications should be available for alerts. - A diagnostics or connectivity view should show which sites are disconnected. - Connectivity and equipment-down conditions should raise alerts. - If a device fails to ping within a configured time period, the system should raise an alert if the controller supports that information. - The client suggested using controller ping behavior as a simple basis for equipment-down alerting. - Thresholds for alarms should be configurable. - A 0.5% threshold was specifically mentioned in the discussion.Fintech Fuel—12860
43Reporting, Terminology & Units - Nozzle-level sales are not clear to the client, partly because of the nomenclature used in the nozzle report. - The transaction report does not clearly mention the unit of measure. - The client wants units such as liters or currency to be visible consistently across the application. - Report terminology and naming should be reviewed so reports are easier for the business to understand. - The client asked the team to review nomenclature used in summarized and detailed reports.Fintech Fuel—8037
44Loss/Gain Analytics & Tank Illustration - The client asked for tank-wise visibility in addition to product-wise views. - The discussion clarified that this requirement is already fulfilled through the Stock Levels report. - The gap identified was client awareness and product discoverability, not missing functionality. - Tank illustration is also required.Fintech Fuel—8037
45Sales Targets & Organizational Hierarchy - Sales targets are maintained at multiple levels, including yearly, quarterly, monthly, and weekly. - The client does not use daily targets as a primary planning level. - Weekly contribution to yearly targets is important. - Targets are established globally for fuel and are often maintained in Excel at area and station level. - The client wants an Excel upload feature so sales targets can be populated in bulk. - A sample Excel file is to be shared by the client after checking with the sales team. - The client also wants targets and sites linked to territory managers and other organizational roles. - The current page can partially support target assignment but does not fully reflect the organizational hierarchy such as region-wise or territory-manager-wise structures. - Further alignment with the sales team is needed to define how targets should be modeled in Fintech Fuel and to set expectations.Fintech Fuel—240112
46Date Range & Time Handling - The client objected to 8 AM to 8 PM reconciliation windows from a UX perspective. - Date filtering should behave as full-day selection: - Single day selection should mean 00:00 to 23:59. - Multi-day selection should mean from the start of the first day to the end of the last day. - Site-level time window configuration exists, but implementation would require client approval. - A possible tolerance buffer of 5% to 10% was discussed to account for timing-related variances such as transactions crossing midnight. - The team agreed that the transaction end time is the correct timestamp to use for calculations.Fintech Fuel—10449
47Price Monitoring & Control - The client wants head-office price communication to each dispenser. - This feature has been built for ALSONs and is in progress for the DOVER Fusion Controller. - If a site changes the selling price from the approved head-office price, Fintech Fuel should raise an alert. - The client gave an example where a site selling at a higher price than the approved price must be flagged. - The client expects exact price matching and does not want a tolerance-based comparison here. - If no transaction has occurred, the current logic may not receive PPU data; this limitation needs a workaround. - Price comparison and price history should be visible in the application. - Price change alerting should not depend only on transactions because the damage may already be done by then. - The team needs to confirm whether the FC controller communicates price changes directly.Fintech Fuel—280131
48Role-Based Access - Ground staff should have read-only access. - Head-office staff should have edit and configuration control. - Manual entry and sensitive corrections should follow user-role restrictions. - The client requested user management roles and an approval system through hierarchy for manual data entry and related control workflows.Fintech Fuel—13663
49Leak & Theft AlertsFintech Fuel—5626
50Sample test for litre quantity from dispenser and then pouring back to tank or detained (discarded)Fintech Fuel—8841
51Get delivery data from OMC companies from their ERP via API and match with how much exact delivery is delivered to Tank at Site to make sure if the Dealer has decanted some delivery to else where or not.Fintech Fuel—9645
52Take manual dip reading with DateTime stamp for opening and closing stocks. And make separate reconciliation report wchih will take manual dip readings entered by user.Fintech Fuel—11252
53Configurable Site Reconn. Time (done)Fintech Fuel—9645

13BULK DEVICE CREATION FOR NON SMART DEVICES (on hold, no further thought process undertaken) Current feature is to bulk create MQTT/IOT devices which has ability to directly connect to Azure IOT hub as an independent devices. It does not cover the schenario where multiple non smart devices are connected to one gateway from where the data is send to cloud. Now need is to replicate such non smart devices (connected to gateway) on OC as logical devices.OmniConnect—4823

SQA
TypeStory Logged39.50 h logged StatusOn Hold PriorityMedium
18-Feb-2026 26-Feb-2026 56.00 h 96.00 h
Testing by Kamran
TypeSub-task Logged39.50 h logged StatusIn-Progress PriorityMedium
40.00 h 6 worklogs
Worklog IDAuthorStartedTime SpentHours
46454 kamran khalid 25-Feb-2026 00:00 3h 3.00 h
46453 kamran khalid 27-Feb-2026 09:00 7h 7.00 h
46452 kamran khalid 02-Mar-2026 09:00 1d 8.00 h
46456 kamran khalid 03-Mar-2026 09:00 1d 8.00 h
46650 kamran khalid 04-Mar-2026 09:11 6h 30m 6.50 h
46822 kamran khalid 05-Mar-2026 09:10 7h 7.00 h
Calculate Tank delivery (Alsons device)
Logged0.00 h logged StatusTo Do PriorityMedium
120.00 h 60.00 h 132.00 h 112.00 h 56.00 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Unified Platform & Multi-Site Visibility (Consolidated view for all sites) - The client wants a unified platform that provides information from all plant sites in one place. - Key data points requested in the unified view include storage tank volume and timestamped deliveries. - The solution must support network-scale visibility for hundreds of sites, not just a small pilot set. - Different user levels, including management, leadership, supervisors, and operations teams, need different views of the same data. - Visibility of sites, targets, and data should follow the assigned user hierarchy.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
208.00 h 104.00 h 228.80 h 194.13 h 97.07 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Deliveries & Delivery Identification - Deliveries are already available with timestamps in Fintech Fuel. - The client wants to distinguish PGL deliveries from non-PGL deliveries, with a clear classification such as PGL and Not PGL. - At COCO sites, current deliveries are considered PGL deliveries. - At DODO sites, the client needs a way to identify who decanted product into storage because multiple providers may use the same storage. - The current level of control at DODO sites is weaker than at COCO sites, and the client wants better controls in Fintech Fuel. - The Deliveries page should use the industry term Delivery Note instead of Bill of Lading. - For imported products, a delivery note or lading reference should be captured on the UI. - A lading reference is currently seen as a reference point only and not as a full control mechanism. - The client wants this requirement captured as a KPI or future control area even if it is initially implemented as a wish-list item. - The system should highlight cases where actual decanted quantity exceeds expected quantity. - Manual delivery references alone are not sufficient to establish end-to-end control.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
144.00 h 72.00 h 158.40 h 134.40 h 67.20 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Dashboard Enhancements - Visual representations - Sales vs target trend must be dynamic not just last 7 days - Stock level must be dynamic not just last 7 days - Remove minute-level filters - Show time of fetched data on stock-level - positive/negative variance with color coding
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
88.00 h 44.00 h 96.80 h 82.13 h 41.07 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Historical Comparison & Trend Analysis - Comparison of different metrices like sales vs deliveries etc.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
144.00 h 72.00 h 158.40 h 134.40 h 67.20 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Reconciliation & Variance Analysis - Reconciliation is a key area for the client, especially to monitor external uplifting. - The reconciliation report should help users see how frequently variation occurs over time. - The client wants anomaly highlighting for variances above selected thresholds. - The current tabulated Excel-like representation is difficult to consume and needs a better UI/UX treatment. - The user wants a product-tile style interaction where clicking a product reveals its reconciliation details. - There is currently no way to define a threshold value in the system; the client asked for configurable selected thresholds. - The system should highlight anomalies at the time of reconciliation. - The reconciliation workflow should support investigation into why a difference exists. - Initial anomaly reasons should at least include data disconnectivity, whether due to internet issues or equipment malfunction. - The reconciliation report must show consistent data and clearly reflect when a site is not transmitting data. - If data is missing, the report should show an exclusion note so the variance can be interpreted correctly. - The client suggested a rule such as flagging any site where no data transmission happens for 60 or more minutes. - Capturing the anomaly event is considered the first priority; the detailed report presentation can be refined with users afterward. - The report should include a summary table on top for the selected period with one row per product, instead of only date-by-date product entries. - The user emphasized that reconciliation UI/UX quality is a possible road blocker.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
272.00 h 136.00 h 299.20 h 253.87 h 126.93 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Data Accuracy, Connectivity & Equipment Failure - Data accuracy is a major concern for the client. - Two primary failure scenarios were identified: -- Internet disconnectivity between the site and the cloud. -- Site hardware or controller malfunction. - In internet disconnectivity scenarios, the controller device must accumulate data and transmit it once connectivity is restored. - No data loss is acceptable when internet connectivity is interrupted. - If the controller or site equipment is not working, the system must raise an alert. - The client wants the system to inform users whether a site cannot reconcile because of internet disconnectivity or equipment malfunction. - If equipment fails and data is not captured, the system must provide manual entry for lost site data. - Manual entry is considered necessary to maintain 100% data accuracy and complete reconciliation. - The client requested an admin-level tool for manual data entry when ATG or related equipment is down. - The discussion referenced a practical case where if ATG is down for two hours, the system should allow offline or manual input. - Offline input data is therefore a required feature.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
304.00 h 152.00 h 334.40 h 283.73 h 141.87 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Alerts, Diagnostics & Notifications - Alerts should not remain isolated in one report; they should connect with diagnostics and related operational reports. - Email notifications should be available for alerts. - A diagnostics or connectivity view should show which sites are disconnected. - Connectivity and equipment-down conditions should raise alerts. - If a device fails to ping within a configured time period, the system should raise an alert if the controller supports that information. - The client suggested using controller ping behavior as a simple basis for equipment-down alerting. - Thresholds for alarms should be configurable. - A 0.5% threshold was specifically mentioned in the discussion.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
128.00 h 64.00 h 140.80 h 119.47 h 59.73 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Reporting, Terminology & Units - Nozzle-level sales are not clear to the client, partly because of the nomenclature used in the nozzle report. - The transaction report does not clearly mention the unit of measure. - The client wants units such as liters or currency to be visible consistently across the application. - Report terminology and naming should be reviewed so reports are easier for the business to understand. - The client asked the team to review nomenclature used in summarized and detailed reports.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
80.00 h 40.00 h 88.00 h 74.67 h 37.33 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Loss/Gain Analytics & Tank Illustration - The client asked for tank-wise visibility in addition to product-wise views. - The discussion clarified that this requirement is already fulfilled through the Stock Levels report. - The gap identified was client awareness and product discoverability, not missing functionality. - Tank illustration is also required.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
80.00 h 40.00 h 88.00 h 74.67 h 37.33 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Sales Targets & Organizational Hierarchy - Sales targets are maintained at multiple levels, including yearly, quarterly, monthly, and weekly. - The client does not use daily targets as a primary planning level. - Weekly contribution to yearly targets is important. - Targets are established globally for fuel and are often maintained in Excel at area and station level. - The client wants an Excel upload feature so sales targets can be populated in bulk. - A sample Excel file is to be shared by the client after checking with the sales team. - The client also wants targets and sites linked to territory managers and other organizational roles. - The current page can partially support target assignment but does not fully reflect the organizational hierarchy such as region-wise or territory-manager-wise structures. - Further alignment with the sales team is needed to define how targets should be modeled in Fintech Fuel and to set expectations.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
240.00 h 120.00 h 264.00 h 224.00 h 112.00 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Date Range & Time Handling - The client objected to 8 AM to 8 PM reconciliation windows from a UX perspective. - Date filtering should behave as full-day selection: - Single day selection should mean 00:00 to 23:59. - Multi-day selection should mean from the start of the first day to the end of the last day. - Site-level time window configuration exists, but implementation would require client approval. - A possible tolerance buffer of 5% to 10% was discussed to account for timing-related variances such as transactions crossing midnight. - The team agreed that the transaction end time is the correct timestamp to use for calculations.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
104.00 h 52.00 h 114.40 h 97.07 h 48.53 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Price Monitoring & Control - The client wants head-office price communication to each dispenser. - This feature has been built for ALSONs and is in progress for the DOVER Fusion Controller. - If a site changes the selling price from the approved head-office price, Fintech Fuel should raise an alert. - The client gave an example where a site selling at a higher price than the approved price must be flagged. - The client expects exact price matching and does not want a tolerance-based comparison here. - If no transaction has occurred, the current logic may not receive PPU data; this limitation needs a workaround. - Price comparison and price history should be visible in the application. - Price change alerting should not depend only on transactions because the damage may already be done by then. - The team needs to confirm whether the FC controller communicates price changes directly.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
280.00 h 140.00 h 308.00 h 261.33 h 130.67 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
Role-Based Access - Ground staff should have read-only access. - Head-office staff should have edit and configuration control. - Manual entry and sensitive corrections should follow user-role restrictions. - The client requested user management roles and an approval system through hierarchy for manual data entry and related control workflows.
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
136.00 h 68.00 h 149.60 h 126.93 h 63.47 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go

OC streaming on Pub Sub
Logged0.00 h logged Status Priority
0.00 h 0.00 h 0.00 h 0.00 h 0.00 h i 0.00 h 0.00 h i

Stories

No child stories were fetched for this epic.
Go
BULK DEVICE CREATION FOR NON SMART DEVICES (on hold, no further thought process undertaken) Current feature is to bulk create MQTT/IOT devices which has ability to directly connect to Azure IOT hub as an independent devices. It does not cover the schenario where multiple non smart devices are connected to one gateway from where the data is send to cloud. Now need is to replicate such non smart devices (connected to gateway) on OC as logical devices.
